verdict.answer_first: B states the core answer immediately and compactly: "Big-O describes how an algorithm's cost grows as its input grows."
verdict.decisiveness: A gives the clearest concrete recommendation: "scan for these two patterns first" followed by nested loops and list membership fixes.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on right now because it gives the same practical traps and fixes with less material to sift through, especially "nested loop to set/dict lookup" and "if x in my_list is O(n)".
